article_meta.title: close Video American Accountability Foundation president releases Education Department 'watch list,' explains why Trump

reasoning: Classification (Right) and confidence are appropriate for a heavily one-sided Fox News piece, but the model repeatedly leans on attributed/quoted language (e.g., 'hoax,' 'local control') as evidence rather than strictly the author’s own prose/structural choices.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: The article’s structure and one-sided framing align with Right; no counterarguments are presented, consistent with Right or Lean Right. 'Right' is a reasonable classification.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: Signals are clear and strong (one-sided framing, outlet, lack of opposing views), so 0.95 confidence is justified.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: Several key indicators rely on language that is attributed to sources (e.g., 'Book Ban Hoax,' 'local control,' 'age-inappropriate/sexually explicit'), which violates the attribution rule, though some structural observations (one-sidedness, outlet/source selection) are valid.
